role: Kurdish-Iraqi academic and mediator; killed in Vienna 1989
bio: Fadel Rasoul was a Kurdish-Iraqi academic who served as a mediator in the negotiations between the [Democratic Party of Iranian Kurdistan (PDKI)](/orgs/democratic-party-of-iranian-kurdistan) and representatives of the Islamic Republic. On 13 July 1989, Rasoul was shot dead in a Vienna apartment alongside PDKI Secretary-General [Abdul Rahman Ghassemlou](/people/abdul-rahman-ghassemlou) and PDKI senior official [Abdullah Ghaderi-Azar](/people/abdullah-ghaderi-azar), in what Austrian and later German investigations established was an assassination carried out by Iranian intelligence operatives. Rasoul was present in his capacity as a trusted neutral facilitator. He was the third victim of the [Ghassemlou assassination](/events/ghassemlou-assassination-1989), and his killing underscored that the Islamic Republic was willing to murder even neutral intermediaries to eliminate Kurdish opposition leadership.
editorial_notes: Killed 13 July 1989 in Vienna alongside Ghassemlou and Ghaderi-Azar. Kurdish-Iraqi academic, present as mediator.
